mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) indicates a robust and expanding economy, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows consistent positive quarter-over-quarter growth, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are predominantly negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which can lead to wage inflation and increased operational costs for corporations, ultimately compressing profit margins and negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE consistently remains below 4.5% for more than 6 months, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are predominantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating and the Federal Reserve responds by increasing the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), this dual signal indicates a hawkish monetary policy stance, increasing borrowing costs and reducing future corporate profitability expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.25% and CPIAUCSL rises by > 0.5% over 3 months
failure_condition: If both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise as specified, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are consistently positive in more than 70% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales (RSAFS) during an economic recovery period signifies strong consumer demand and spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and profits, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows strong growth during a declared recovery period, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are flat or negative in the majority of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below a critical level indicates a contraction in manufacturing and broader economic activity, signaling an impending slowdown that negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial output is a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 1.0% over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and overall economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive month-over-month growth but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are predominantly negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates a weakening housing market and broader economic slowdown due to tighter financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as future growth prospects diminish.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by > 10% month-over-month AND FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.25% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:housing is sensitive to interest rates
failure_condition: If HOUST declines significantly while FEDFUNDS rises as specified, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low inflation (CPIAUCSL) during periods of economic weakness can signal deflationary pressures and weak demand, prompting central banks to maintain accommodative policies or even cut rates, which can be interpreted as a positive for equities as discount rates remain low.
evidence_refs: fact:low inflation can lead to lower rates
regime_hint: low inflation environment during economic weakness
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL remains below 1.0% annual change during periods of economic weakness, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are consistently negative.
